scdm_tables.description: The SCDM Enrollment Table has a start/stop structure that contains one record per continuous enrollment period. Patients with medical coverage, drug coverage, or both should be included. A unique combination of PatID, Enr_Start, Enr_End, MedCov, DrugCov, Chart, PlanType, and PayerType identifies a unique record. A break in enrollment (of at least one day) or a change in either the medical or drug coverage variables should generate a new record.

variables.name: PatID
variables.definition: Arbitrary person-level identifier. Used to link across tables. A new enrollment period generates a new record, but the same person should have the same PatID on subsequent records. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: PatID is a pseudo-identifier with a consistent crosswalk to the true identifier retained by the source Data Partner. For analytical data sets requiring patient-level data, only the pseudo-identifier is used to link across all information belonging to a patient.

variables.name: Enr_Start
variables.definition: Date of the beginning of the enrollment period. If the exact date is unknown, use the first day of the month. Enr_Start should not be before January 1, 2000.
notes: Adjacent and overlapping enrollment periods with the same PatID, Enr_Start, Enr_End, MedCov, DrugCov, and Chart values should be collapsed. Enrollment periods separated by more than one day should not be bridged. For example, an Enr_End date of 1/31/2019 should be bridged with an Enr_Start date of 2/1/2019, but should not be bridged with an Enr_Start date of 2/2/2019.

For instances where an overlapping enrollment span occurs due to the PlanType or PayerType variables, both spans should have the value of that variable set to Other, and be collapsed into a single span.

variables.name: Enr_End
variables.definition: Date of the end of the enrollment period. If the exact date is unknown, use the last day of the month.
notes: Adjacent and overlapping enrollment periods with the same PatID, Enr_Start, Enr_End, MedCov, DrugCov, and Chart values should be collapsed. Enrollment periods separated by more than one day should not be bridged.
Enr_End should not be imputed using the date of death found in the Death table.

variables.name: MedCov
variables.definition: Mark as Y if the health plan has any responsibility for covering medical care for the member during this enrollment period (i.e., if you expect to observe medical care provided to this member during the enrollment period). Mark as A if only ambulatory visit coverage is provided.

variables.name: DrugCov
variables.definition: Mark as Y if the health plan has any responsibility for covering outpatient prescription drugs for the member during this enrollment period (i.e., if you expect to observe outpatient pharmacy dispensings for this member during this enrollment period).

variables.name: Chart
variables.definition: Chart abstraction flag to answer the question, "Are you able to request charts for this member?" This flag does not address chart availability. Mark as Y if there are no contractual restrictions between you and the member (or sponsor) that would prohibit you from requesting any chart for this member.
notes: Chart variable aims to identify enrollment periods for which medical charts cannot be requested. Potential scenarios include: Charts cannot be requested for Medicare members (all enrollment periods for Medicare members should be assigned Chart=N), Charts cannot be requested for administrative services only (ASO) populations (all ASO enrollment periods should be assigned Chart=N). If there is no definitive information indicating that medical charts cannot be requested for member enrollment period(s), records should be assigned Chart=Y.

variables.name: PlanType
variables.definition: Indicate the type of insurance plan coverage for the patient during the enrollment span.
notes: Adjacent and overlapping enrollment periods with the same PatID, Enr_Start, Enr_End, MedCov, DrugCov, and Chart values should be collapsed.

For instances where an overlapping enrollment span occurs due to the PlanType or PayerType variables, both spans should have the value of that variable set to Other, and be collapsed into a single span.

variables.name: PayerType
variables.definition: Indicate the type of payer for the patient's plan during the enrollment span.
notes: Adjacent and overlapping enrollment periods with the same PatID, Enr_Start, Enr_End, MedCov, DrugCov, and Chart values should be collapsed.

For instances where an overlapping enrollment span occurs due to the PlanType or PayerType variables, both spans should have the value of that variable set to Other, and be collapsed into a single span.

scdm_tables.description: The SCDM Demographic Table contains one record per PatID with the most recent information on Birth_Date, Sex, Race, PostalCode, ImputedRace, ImputedHispanic, and Hispanic.

variables.name: PatID
variables.definition: Arbitrary person-level identifier. Used to link across tables. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: PatID is a pseudo-identifier with a consistent crosswalk to the true identifier retained by the source Data Partner. For analytical data sets requiring patient-level data, only the pseudo-identifier is used to link across all information belonging to a patient.

variables.name: Birth_Date
variables.definition: Date of birth.

variables.name: Sex
variables.definition: Sex.
notes: At this time the SCDM retains one Race and one Hispanic value per individual.

variables.name: Hispanic
variables.definition: A person of Cuban, Mexican, Puerto Rican, South or Central American, or other Spanish culture or origin, regardless of race.

variables.name: Race

values.description: Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander

variables.definition: Please use only one race value per patient.
notes: Data Partners not imputing a value for Race or Hispanic Status should leave ImputedRace or ImputedHispanic missing.

variables.name: PostalCode
variables.definition: USA: First 5 digits of the ZIP code of the patient's most recent primary residence. (5 characters only) Other: Complete postal code or region identifier. (variable length is dependent on code length)

variables.name: PostalCode_Date
variables.definition: Earliest date that the PostalCode is believed to be continuously correct up until the end date of your source data for the ETL. Date will be updated/overwritten as postal code for a patient/member changes over time.

variables.name: ImputedRace

values.description: Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander

notes: Data Partners may populate ImputedRace or ImputedHispanic, regardless of the value of Race or Hispanic.

variables.name: ImputedHispanic
variables.definition: A person of Cuban, Mexican, Puerto Rican, South or Central American, or other Spanish culture or origin, regardless of race.
notes: Data Partners may populate ImputedRace or ImputedHispanic, regardless of the value of Race or Hispanic.

scdm_tables.description: The SCDM Outpatient Pharmacy Dispensing Table contains one record per unique outpatient pharmacy dispensing. Rollback transactions and other adjustments should be processed before populating this table.

variables.name: PatID
variables.definition: Arbitrary person-level identifier. Used to link across tables. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: PatID is a pseudo-identifier with a consistent crosswalk to the true identifier retained by the source Data Partner. For analytical data sets requiring patient-level data, only the pseudo-identifier is used to link across all information belonging to a patient.

variables.name: ProviderID
variables.definition: Identifier for the individual provider who prescribed the drug. As with the PatID, the provider identifier is a pseudo-identifier with a consistent crosswalk to the real identifier. If an individual provider/prescriber can not be identified, set to special missing value .U. This variable links to the Provider table. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: If all ProviderID values are unknown (i.e., set to .U), use Num(3) for the length of the variable.

variables.name: RxDate
variables.definition: Dispensing date (as close as possible to date the person received the dispensing).

variables.name: Rx
variables.definition: Code representing a prescribed medication or medical device. Please expunge any place holders (e.g., '-' or extra digit). Use the fewest number of bytes to contain values for all values of Rx_CodeType.

variables.name: Rx_CodeType
variables.definition: Code type of prescribed medication or medical device. This field combined with the Rx field should be used to capture any type of prescribed medication or medical device available in the source data. Other code types may be added as new terminologies are used.
notes: Rx_CodeType includes options for values that are relevant to medication and medical device codes used in different international jurisdictions.

variables.name: RxSup
variables.definition: Number of days that the medication supports based on the number of doses as reported by the pharmacist. This amount is typically found on the dispensings record. It should not be necessary to calculate this variable for use in the SCDM. Positive integer values are expected.
notes: Rollback transactions and other adjustments that are indicative of a dispensing being canceled or not picked up by the member should be processed before populating this table.

variables.name: RxAmt
variables.definition: Number of units (pills, tablets, vials) dispensed. Net amount per dispensing. This amount is typically found on the dispensings record. It should not be necessary to calculate this variable for use in the SCDM. Positive values are expected.
notes: Rollback transactions and other adjustments that are indicative of a dispensing being canceled or not picked up by the member should be processed before populating this table.

scdm_tables.description: The SCDM Encounter Table contains one record per PatID and EncounterID. Each encounter should have a single record in the SCDM Encounter Table. Each diagnosis and procedure recorded during the encounter should have a separate record in the Diagnosis or Procedure Tables. Rollback transactions and other adjustments should be processed before populating this table.

variables.name: PatID
variables.definition: Arbitrary person-level identifier. Used to link across tables. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: PatID is a pseudo-identifier with a consistent crosswalk to the true identifier retained by the source Data Partner. For analytical data sets requiring patient-level data, only the pseudo-identifier is used to link across all information belonging to a patient.

variables.name: EncounterID
variables.definition: Arbitrary encounter-level identifier. Used to link across the Encounter, Diagnosis, Procedure, Vital Signs, Mother-Infant Linkage, Inpatient Pharmacy, Inpatient Transfusion, Prescribing, and Patient-Reported Measures Response tables. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: For efficiency, medical utilization data is captured in 3 tables: Encounter, Diagnosis, and Procedure. The 3 tables can be used independently or in combination, depending on the protocol.

variables.name: ADate
variables.definition: Encounter or admission date.

variables.name: DDate
variables.missing_allowed: Yes, conditional on EncType value. If EncType = IP or IS, only SAS special missing .S is allowed.
variables.definition: Discharge date. Should be populated for all Inpatient Hospital Stay (IP) and Non-Acute Institutional Stay (IS) encounter types. May be populated for Emergency Department (ED) encounter types. Should be missing for ambulatory visit (AV or OA) encounter types. For patients still in facility (IP or IS) use special missing value of .S.

variables.name: EncType
variables.definition: Encounter Type

variables.name: FacilityID
variables.definition: Local facility pseudo-identifier that identifies hospital or clinic in which the encounter occurred. There must be only one facility per encounter. Used for chart abstraction and validation. This variable links to the Facility table. If unavailable, set to special missing value .U. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: If all FacilityID values are unknown (i.e., set to .U), use Num(3) for the length of the variable.

variables.name: Discharge_Disposition
variables.definition: Should be populated for Inpatient Hospital Stay (IP) and Non-Acute Institutional Stay (IS) encounter types. May be populated for Emergency Department (ED) encounter types. Should be missing for ambulatory visit (AV or OA) encounter types.

variables.name: Discharge_Status
variables.definition: Should be populated for Inpatient Hospital Stay (IP) and Non-Acute Institutional Stay (IS) encounter types. May be populated for Emergency Department (ED) encounter types. Should be missing for ambulatory visit (AV or OA) encounter types.
notes: Guidance regarding Discharge_Status is attached in the Discharge Status Code Guidance document.

variables.name: DRG
variables.definition: Diagnosis Related Group. Should be populated for IP and IS encounter types. May be populated for ED encounter types. Can be missing for IP, IS, ED. Should be missing for AV or OA encounters. Use leading zeroes for codes less than 100.

variables.name: DRG_Type
variables.definition: DRG code version. MS-DRG (current system) began on 10/1/2007. Should be missing for AV or OA encounters. Can be missing for IP, IS, ED.

variables.name: Admitting_Source
variables.definition: Should be populated for Inpatient Hospital Stay (IP) and Non-Acute Institutional Stay (IS) encounter types. May be populated for Emergency Department (ED) encounter types. Should be missing for ambulatory visit (AV or OA) encounter types.

scdm_tables.description: The SCDM Diagnosis Table contains one record per unique combination of PatID, EncounterID, DX_CodeType, DX, and ProviderID. This table should capture all uniquely recorded diagnoses for all encounters, per provider.

variables.name: PatID
variables.definition: Arbitrary person-level identifier. Used to link across tables. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: PatID is a pseudo-identifier with a consistent crosswalk to the true identifier retained by the source Data Partner. For analytical data sets requiring patient-level data, only the pseudo-identifier is used to link across all information belonging to a patient.

variables.name: EncounterID
variables.definition: Arbitrary encounter-level identifier. Used to link across the Encounter, Diagnosis, Procedure, Vital Signs, Mother-Infant Linkage, Inpatient Pharmacy, Inpatient Transfusion, and Prescribing tables. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: For efficiency medical utilization data is captured in 3 tables: Encounter, Diagnosis, and Procedure. The 3 tables can be used independently or in combination, depending on the protocol.

variables.name: ADate
variables.definition: Encounter or admission date; Value must match ADate in the Encounter table for the same PatID/ EncounterID combination.

variables.name: ProviderID
variables.definition: Identifier for the individual provider who made the diagnosis. As with the PatID, the ProviderID identifier is a pseudo-identifier with a consistent crosswalk to the real identifier. If an individual provider cannot be identified, populate the ProviderID value with special missing value of .U. This variable links to the Provider table. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: If all ProviderID values are unknown (i.e., set to .U), use Num(3) for the length of the variable.

variables.name: EncType
variables.definition: Encounter Type

variables.name: DX
variables.definition: Remove decimal points, site specific suffixes and prefixes. Other codes should be listed as recorded in the source data. Use the fewest number of bytes to contain values for all values of DX_CodeType.

variables.name: Dx_Codetype
variables.definition: Diagnosis code type. This field combined with the DX field should be used to capture any type of diagnosis or clinical concept available in the source data. Other code types may be added as new terminologies are used.

variables.name: OrigDX
variables.values: Original diagnosis from source table, if different
variables.definition: Used if Data Partner has to map internal codes to standard codes.

variables.name: PDX
variables.definition: Principal discharge diagnosis flag. Populate only for IP and IS encounters. For ED, AV, and OA encounter types, set as missing/blank. One principal diagnosis is expected per Encounter, although in some instances more than one diagnosis may be flagged as principal.

variables.name: PAdmit
variables.definition: Indicates whether the diagnosis code is indicative of a condition present at admission.
notes: Sentinel Data Partners receive individual guidance for populating the PAdmit field.

scdm_tables.description: The SCDM Procedure Table contains one record per unique combination of PatID, EncounterID, Px_CodeType, Px, and ProviderID. This table should capture all uniquely recorded procedures for all encounters, per provider.

variables.name: PatID
variables.definition: Arbitrary person-level identifier. Used to link across tables. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: PatID is a pseudo-identifier with a consistent crosswalk to the true identifier retained by the source Data Partner. For analytical data sets requiring patient-level data, only the pseudo-identifier is used to link across all information belonging to a patient.

variables.name: EncounterID
variables.definition: Arbitrary encounter-level identifier. Used to link across the Encounter, Diagnosis, Procedure, Vital Signs, Mother-Infant Linkage, Inpatient Pharmacy, Inpatient Transfusion, Prescribing, and Patient-Reported Measures Response tables. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: For efficiency medical utilization data is captured in 3 tables: Encounter, Diagnosis, and Procedure. The 3 tables can be used independently or in combination, depending on the protocol.

variables.name: ADate
variables.definition: Encounter or admission date; Value must match ADate in the Encounter table for the same PatID/ EncounterID combination.

variables.name: ProviderID
variables.definition: Identifier for the individual provider who made the diagnosis. As with the PatID, the ProviderID identifier is a pseudo-identifier with a consistent crosswalk to the real identifier. If an individual provider cannot be identified, populate the ProviderID value with special missing value of .U. This variable links to the Provider table. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: If all ProviderID values are unknown (i.e., set to .U), use Num(3) for the length of the variable.

variables.name: EncType
variables.definition: Encounter Type

variables.name: PX
variables.definition: Remove decimal points, site specific suffixes and prefixes. Other codes should be listed as recorded in the source data. Convert local codes to standard codes. Use the fewest number of bytes to contain values for all values of Px_CodeType.

variables.name: PX_CodeType
variables.definition: Procedure code type.

variables.name: OrigPX
variables.values: Original procedure code from source table, if different.
variables.definition: Used if Data Partner has to map internal codes to standard codes.

scdm_tables.description: The SCDM Prescribing Table contains one record per PatID and PrescribingID. This table should capture all uniquely recorded prescription orders, as unique combination of PatID, OrderDate, Rx_CodeType, Rx, and RxRefills.

variables.name: PatID
variables.definition: Arbitrary person-level identifier. Used to link across tables. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: PatID is a pseudo-identifier with a consistent crosswalk to the true identifier retained by the source Data Partner. For analytical data sets requiring patient-level data, only the pseudo-identifier is used to link across all information belonging to a patient.

variables.name: EncounterID
variables.definition: Arbitrary encounter-level identifier. Used to link across the Encounter, Diagnosis, Procedure, Vital Signs, Mother-Infant Linkage, Inpatient Pharmacy, Inpatient Transfusion, and Prescribing tables. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: The Encounter, Diagnosis, Procedure, Inpatient Transfusion, Inpatient Pharmacy, Vital Signs, Mother-Infant Linkage, and Prescribing tables are linked by PatID and EncounterID.

variables.name: PrescribingID
variables.definition: Arbitrary identifier for each unique Prescribing record. Does not need to be persistent across refreshes, and may be created by any method that achieve a unique identifier per row. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: The Encounter, Diagnosis, Procedure, Inpatient Transfusion, Inpatient Pharmacy, Vital Signs, Mother-Infant Linkage, and Prescribing tables are linked by PatID and EncounterID.

variables.name: ProviderID
variables.values: Unique provider identifier or Special Missing .U for unknown
variables.definition: Provider code for the individual provider who prescribed the medication. The provider code is a pseudo-identifier with a consistent crosswalk to the real identifier and is the same set of pseudo-identifiers for ProviderID in other SCDM tables. If an individual provider can not be identified, populate the ProviderID value with special missing value of .U. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: If all ProviderID values are unknown (i.e., set to .U), use Num(3) for the length of the variable.

variables.name: OrderDate
variables.definition: Order date of the prescription by the provider

variables.name: Rx_CodeType
variables.definition: Code type of prescribed medication or medical device. This field combined with the Rx field should be used to capture any type of prescribed medication or medical device available in the source data. Other code types may be added as new terminologies are used.

variables.name: Rx
variables.definition: Code representing a prescribed medication or medical device. Please expunge any place holders (e.g., '-' or extra digit). Use the fewest number of bytes to contain values for all values of Rx_CodeType.

variables.name: RxAmt
variables.values: Positive integer values or Special Missing .U for unknown
variables.definition: Quantity ordered.

variables.name: RxSup
variables.values: Positive integer values or Special Missing .U for unknown
variables.definition: Number of days supply ordered, as specified by the prescription.

variables.name: RxSource
variables.definition: Source of the prescribing information.

variables.name: RxRoute
variables.definition: Route of medication delivery.

variables.name: RxDoseQuantity
variables.values: Values greater than zero or Special Missing .U for unknown
variables.definition: Dose of a given medication, as ordered by the provider.

variables.name: RxDoseUnit
variables.definition: Units of measure associated with the dose of the medication (i.e., RxDoseQuantity) as ordered by the provider.

variables.name: RxDoseForm
variables.definition: The unit associated with the quantity prescribed. This is equivalent to RxNorm Dose Form. Only valuable if using RxNorm (Rx_CodeType = RN). Can be blank.

variables.name: RxFreqQuantity
variables.values: Number per unit in RxFreqUnit; integers or Special Missing .U for unknown
variables.definition: Specified frequency of medication per unit of time.

variables.name: RxFreqUnit
variables.definition: Frequency unit of administration.

variables.name: RxRefills
variables.values: 0, positive integers, or Special Missing .U for unknown
variables.definition: Number of refills ordered (not including the original prescription). If no refills are ordered, the value should be zero.

variables.name: RxPrnFlag
variables.definition: Flag to indicate that all or part of medication frequency instructions includes "as needed."

variables.name: RxDAW
variables.definition: Flag to indicate whether the provider indicated that the medication order was to be dispensed as written (DAW).

scdm_tables.description: The SCDM Facility Table contains one record per FacilityID. This table should capture all uniquely recorded facility identifiers in the Encounter table and the Laboratory Results (if populated) tables.

variables.name: FacilityID
variables.definition: Local facility pseudo-identifier that identifies hospital or clinic. Taken from facility claims. Used for chart abstraction and validation.
notes: FacilityID links to the same-named variable in the Encounter and Laboratory Results table and must be the same length across both tables.

variables.name: Facility_Location
variables.values: Geographic location (postal/region code) of servicing facility (not billing location)
variables.definition: Should be left blank if missing.

scdm_tables.description: The SCDM Provider Table contains one record per ProviderID. This table should capture all uniquely recorded individual provider identifiers in the Dispensing, Diagnosis, Procedure, and Prescribing (if populated) tables.

variables.name: ProviderID
variables.definition: Identifier for the individual provider who wrote a prescription in the Prescribing table, was identified in a row in the Dispensing table, made a diagnosis in the Diagnosis table, or performed a procedure or service in the Procedure table. As with the PatID, the provider identifier is a pseudo-identifier with a consistent crosswalk to the real identifier. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: ProviderID links to the same-named variable in the Dispensing, Diagnosis, Procedure, and if populated, the Prescribing tables. ProviderID must be the same length across all tables.

variables.name: Specialty
variables.values: Provider specialty, See PVD.specialty table within Reference Tables.
variables.definition: Code indicating the medical specialty of the Provider. If an individual provider has more than one specialty, select the specialty under which the provider practices most often. Data Partners are requested to map to the most specific description possible. That is, Map to a 10-character Taxonomy Code if possible; otherwise use a 2-character specialty code. Length of either 2 or 10 per row, aligning with Specialty_CodeType. Use 99 for unknown or undefined individual provider specialty.
notes: CMS Provider Taxonomy & Specialty Codes: Use only those values found in "PVD.specialty" Table, located within the Reference Tables. These are a subset from the CMS Taxonomy Crosswalk.

variables.name: Specialty_CodeType
variables.definition: 2 = a 2-character code as found in the "PVD.specialty" Table within the Reference Tables. 0 = a 10-character code as found in the "PVD.specialty" Table within the Reference Tables.

scdm_tables.description: The SCDM Laboratory Result Table contains one record per result/entry. Include only resulted lab tests. Data Partners are strongly encouraged to review the comprehensive Sentinel Common Data Model Laboratory Result Table Documentation for details on how to populate each variable.

variables.name: PatID
variables.definition: Arbitrary person-level identifier. Used to link across tables. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: PatID is a pseudo-identifier with a consistent crosswalk to the true identifier retained by the source Data Partner. For analytical data sets requiring patient-level data, only the pseudo-identifier is used to link across all information belonging to a patient.

variables.name: LabID
variables.definition: Useful to map back to source data. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.

variables.name: MS_Test_Name
variables.values: See LAB.ms_test_name within the Reference Tables.
variables.definition: Abbreviated test name. Populated for all records. Several LOINC codes (LOINC) and/or local codes (LOCAL_CD) can point to one MS_Test_Name.

variables.name: Result_Type
variables.definition: Indicates whether the laboratory test result in Data Partner's source data is Numeric (e.g., 100 ug/mL, <100 ug/mL, or >100 ug/mL) or Character (e.g., +, POS, POSITIVE, or ranges, such as 50-100 mg/mL). Unknown value is populated when MS_Test_Name = Unmapped

variables.name: MS_Test_Sub_Category
variables.definition: Sub-category for specific test names only.

variables.name: Fast_Ind
variables.definition: Fasting Indicator. Not all laboratory tests are differentiated by fasting status.

variables.name: Specimen_Source
variables.definition: Specimen source. Populated for all records.

variables.name: LOINC
variables.definition: Logical Observation Identifiers, Names, and Codes.
notes: Includes hyphen; no leading zeros removed

variables.name: Stat
variables.definition: Immediacy of test. Determines if routine or emergent.

variables.name: Pt_Loc
variables.definition: Patient location where the lab specimen was obtained.

variables.name: Result_Loc
variables.definition: Location of the test result. Default is L.

variables.name: LOCAL_CD
variables.definition: Local code (non-LOINC) related to an individual lab test. For local programmer use only.

variables.name: BATTERY_CD
variables.definition: Local code related to a battery or panel of lab tests. For local programmer use only.

variables.name: PX
variables.definition: Procedure Code associated with the laboratory result record.

variables.name: PX_CodeType
variables.definition: Procedure code type associated with the laboratory result record.

variables.name: Order_dt
variables.definition: Date that the test was ordered.

variables.name: Lab_dt
variables.definition: Date that the specimen was collected.
notes: Most relevant date variable for Sentinel activities

variables.name: Lab_tm
variables.definition: Time of day that the specimen was collected.

variables.name: Result_dt
variables.definition: Date that the laboratory test was resulted.

variables.name: Result_tm
variables.definition: Time that the laboratory test was resulted.

variables.name: Orig_Result
variables.definition: Original result value, stripped of modifiers and units for numeric results; reflects source data for character results.

variables.name: MS_Result_C
variables.definition: Standardized result value for character results only.

variables.name: MS_Result_N
variables.definition: Standardized/converted numeric result for numeric types.

variables.name: Modifier
variables.definition: Modifier for result values, reflecting relational operators.

variables.name: Orig_Result_unit
variables.definition: Original units for the test result, as reported in source data.

variables.name: Std_Result_unit
variables.definition: Standardized units as intermediary step to MS_Result_unit.

variables.name: MS_Result_unit
variables.definition: Converted/standardized result units for MS_Result_N values. Set to UNKNOWN when missing.

variables.name: Norm_Range_low
variables.definition: Lower bound of normal reference range assigned by laboratory.

variables.name: Modifier_low
variables.definition: Modifier for Norm_Range_low values.

variables.name: Norm_Range_high
variables.definition: Upper bound of normal reference range assigned by laboratory.

variables.name: Modifier_high
variables.definition: Modifier for Norm_Range_high values.

variables.name: Abn_ind
variables.definition: Abnormal result indicator from source data, not programmatically derived.

variables.name: Order_dept
variables.definition: Local code for ordering provider department. Populate only if available.

variables.name: FacilityID
variables.definition: Local facility pseudo-identifier identifying hospital or clinic. Set to .U if unavailable.

scdm_tables.description: The SCDM Vital Signs Table contains one record per result/entry.

variables.name: PatID
variables.definition: Arbitrary person-level identifier. Used to link across tables. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: PatID is a pseudo-identifier with a consistent crosswalk to the true identifier retained by the source Data Partner. For analytical data sets requiring patient-level data, only the pseudo-identifier is used to link across all information belonging to a patient.

variables.name: EncounterID
variables.definition: Arbitrary encounter-level identifier. Used to link acronss the Encounter, Diagnosis, Procedure, Vital Signs, Mother-Infant Linkage, Inpatient Pharmacy, Inpatient Transfusion, Prescribing, and Patient-Reported Measures Response tables. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: The Encounter, Diagnosis, Procedure, Inpatient Transfusion, Inpatient Pharmacy, Vital Signs, Mother-Infant Linkage, and Prescribing tables are linked by PatID and EncounterID.

variables.name: Measure_Date
variables.definition: Date the vital signs were measured.

variables.name: Measure_Time
variables.definition: Time associated with the vital signs record. This may be the time an actual blood pressure measurement was taken or it may be a check-in time from encounter.

variables.name: HT
variables.definition: Height (in inches). ####.## = If HT can be represented in inches. Only populated if height was taken on this date. If missing, leave blank.

variables.name: WT
variables.definition: Weight (in lbs). ####.## = If WT can be represented in pounds. Only populated if weight was taken on this date. If missing, leave blank.

variables.name: Diastolic
variables.definition: Diastolic blood pressure. ### = If Diastolic can be represented in mmHg. Only populated if diastolic blood pressure was taken on this date. If missing, leave blank.

variables.name: Systolic
variables.definition: Systolic blood pressure. ### = If Systolic can be represented in mmHg. Only populated if systolic blood pressure was taken on this date. If missing, leave blank.

variables.name: BP_Type
variables.definition: Type of blood pressure taken.

variables.name: Position
variables.definition: Position for orthostatic blood pressure. If unknown, leave blank.

variables.name: Tobacco
variables.definition: Tobacco status as of the visit date. Unknown values should be left blank. The "Not asked" value should be used only when it is a valid response from your system (e.g. this is a valid value for EPIC). The "Conflicting" value should be used when you receive tobacco information from multiple sources that disagree.

variables.name: Tobacco_Type
variables.definition: Type of tobacco used. Unknown values should be left blank.

scdm_tables.description: The SCDM Death Table contains one record per PatID. When legacy data have conflicting reports, please make a local determination as to which to use. There is typically a 1-2 year lag in death registry data.

variables.name: PatID
variables.definition: Arbitrary person-level identifier. Used to link across tables. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: PatID is a pseudo-identifier with a consistent crosswalk to the true identifier retained by the source Data Partner. For analytical data sets requiring patient-level data, only the pseudo-identifier is used to link across all information belonging to a patient.

variables.name: DeathDt
variables.definition: Date of death.

variables.name: DtImpute
variables.definition: When DeathDt is imputed, this variable indicates which parts of the date were imputed.

variables.name: Source

values.description: Death files from State/ Region/ Province

variables.definition: Source of death information.

variables.name: Confidence
variables.definition: Confidence that the patient drawn from the Source data represents the actual patient (contrasts with Confidence in the Cause of Death table). If uncertain, use P = Poor.

scdm_tables.description: The SCDM Cause of Death Table can contain multiple COD records per PatID. When legacy data have conflicting reports, please make a local determination as to which to use. There is typically a 1-2 year lag in death registry data.

variables.name: PatID
variables.definition: Arbitrary person-level identifier. Used to link across tables. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: PatID is a pseudo-identifier with a consistent crosswalk to the true identifier retained by the source Data Partner. For analytical data sets requiring patient-level data, only the pseudo-identifier is used to link across all information belonging to a patient.

variables.name: COD
variables.definition: Remove decimal points, site specific suffixes and prefixes.

variables.name: CodeType

values.description: National Vital Statistics Registry Death Code

variables.definition: Cause of death code type.

variables.name: CauseType
variables.definition: Cause of death type. There should be only one "Underlying" code for cause of death, per PatID.

variables.name: Source

values.description: Death files from State/ Region/ Province

variables.definition: Source of cause of death information.

variables.name: Confidence
variables.definition: Confidence that the patient drawn from the Source data represents the actual patient (contrasts with Confidence in the Death table). If uncertain, use P = Poor.

scdm_tables.description: The SCDM Inpatient Pharmacy table contains data on inpatient drug administrations. It contains one record per unique combination of PatID, NDC, RxADate, RxATime, and RxID. Each record represents a unique inpatient pharmacy dispensing administration.

variables.name: PatID
variables.definition: Arbitrary person-level identifier. Used to link across tables. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: PatID is a pseudo-identifier with a consistent crosswalk to the true identifier retained by the source Data Partner. For analytical data sets requiring patient-level data, only the pseudo-identifier is used to link across all information belonging to a patient.

variables.name: EncounterID
variables.definition: Arbitrary encounter-level identifier. Used to link across the Encounter, Diagnosis, Procedure, Vital Signs, Mother-Infant Linkage, Inpatient Pharmacy, Inpatient Transfusion, and Prescribing tables. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: The Encounter, Diagnosis, Procedure, Inpatient Transfusion, Inpatient Pharmacy, Vital Signs, Mother-Infant Linkage, and Prescribing tables are linked by PatID and EncounterID.

variables.name: NDC
variables.definition: Please expunge any place holders (e.g., '-' or extra digit)

variables.name: RxID
variables.definition: Useful to map back to source data. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.

variables.name: RxADate
variables.definition: Rx Administration date

variables.name: RxATime
variables.definition: Rx Administration time

variables.name: RxRoute
variables.values: Actual route description from source system
variables.definition: Actual / administered route. Standard list of allowable values under development.

variables.name: RxDose
variables.values: Numeric digits with or without a decimal
variables.definition: Actual/administered dose. Intended to be analyzed in conjunction with the RxUOM (unit of measure) field and product strength data associated with the Drug Code (available from drug databases). Format captures maximum # of whole and decimal digits allowed by software technology for numeric data.

variables.name: RxUOM
variables.values: Actual unit of measure from source system
variables.definition: Actual/administered unit of measure. Intended to be analyzed in conjunction with the RxDose field and product strength data associated with the Drug Code (available from drug databases). Standard list of allowable values under development.

scdm_tables.description: The SCDM Inpatient Transfusion table contains data on inpatient blood transfusion administrations. It contains one record per unique combination of PatID and TransID. Each record represents a unique inpatient pharmacy transfusion administration, as defined by unique value combinations of PatID/ TransCode/ TransCode_Type/ TDate_Start/ Ttime_Start.

variables.name: PatID
variables.definition: Arbitrary person-level identifier. Used to link across tables. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: PatID is a pseudo-identifier with a consistent crosswalk to the true identifier retained by the source Data Partner. For analytical data sets requiring patient-level data, only the pseudo-identifier is used to link across all information belonging to a patient.

variables.name: EncounterID
variables.definition: Arbitrary encounter-level identifier. Used to link across the Encounter, Diagnosis, Procedure, Vital Signs, Mother-Infant Linkage, Inpatient Pharmacy, Inpatient Transfusion, and Prescribing tables. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: The Encounter, Diagnosis, Procedure, Inpatient Transfusion, Inpatient Pharmacy, Vital Signs, Mother-Infant Linkage, and Prescribing tables are linked by PatID and EncounterID.

variables.name: TransID
variables.definition: Retain because useful to map back to source data. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.

variables.name: TransCode
variables.values: Code value for an infusion product
variables.definition: Must be paired with the correct TransCode_Type

variables.name: TransCode_Type
variables.definition: Transfusion product code type. This variable combined with the TransCode variable should be used to capture any type of Inpatient Infusion product in the source data. Other code types will be added as new terminologies are used.

variables.name: Orig_TransProd
variables.definition: Name of product within Data Partner. Site-specific length.

variables.name: BloodType

values.code: AB (upper case) with Rh factor (+, -, or null only) or UN

variables.definition: Blood type and Rh factors, without leading spaces. Convert any text Rh factor to symbols (e.g., "pos" to "+", "negative" to "-"). Rh factor can be blank.

variables.name: TDate_Start
variables.definition: Administration start date.

variables.name: TTime_Start
variables.definition: Administration start time.

variables.name: TDate_End
variables.definition: Administration end date.

variables.name: TTime_End
variables.definition: Administration end time.

variables.name: EncType
variables.definition: Encounter Type

scdm_tables.description: The SCDM Mother-Infant Linkage Table contains one record per MPatID, CPatID, and ADate. This table is created following identification of mothers (via evidence of live birth delivery by individuals between 10 and 54 years of age as of the admission date of the delivery encounter and has an assigned value of Female in the Sentinel Common Data Model demographic table variable Sex) and infants (via date of birth) in the Sentinel Distributed Database (SDD).

variables.name: MPatID
variables.missing_allowed: Missing only allowed for Infant Records
variables.definition: Arbitrary person-level identifier. Used to link across tables. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table. Must match mother PatID value in all other SCDM tables. Blank for infant-only records.
notes: PatID is a pseudo-identifier with a consistent crosswalk to the true identifier retained by the source Data Partner. For analytical data sets requiring patient-level data, only the pseudo-identifier is used to link across all information belonging to a patient.

variables.name: MBirth_Date
variables.missing_allowed: Missing only allowed for Infant Records
variables.definition: Mother Birth_Date value from SCDM Demographic table. Missing for infant-only records.

variables.name: Age
variables.missing_allowed: Missing only allowed for Infant Records
variables.definition: Mother's age as of ADate. Missing for infant-only records.

variables.name: EncounterID
variables.missing_allowed: Missing only allowed for Infant Records
variables.definition: Arbitrary encounter-level identifier. Used to link across the Encounter, Diagnosis, Procedure, Vital Signs, Mother-Infant Linkage, Inpatient Pharmacy, Inpatient Transfusion, Prescribing, and Patient-Reported Measures Response tables. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table. EncounterID value from SCDM Encounter table, for mother's delivery encounter. Missing for infant-only records.
notes: The Encounter, Diagnosis, Procedure, Inpatient Transfusion, Inpatient Pharmacy, Vital Signs, Mother-Infant Linkage, and Prescribing tables are linked by PatID and EncounterID.

variables.name: EncType
variables.missing_allowed: Missing only allowed for Infant Records
variables.definition: EncType value from SCDM Encounter table, for mother's delivery encounter. Blank for infant-only records.
notes: If more than 1 delivery encounter occur on the same ADate, then the EncType values are based on the encounter selected from the following hierarchy: IP > ED > AV > IS > OA.

variables.name: ADate
variables.missing_allowed: Missing only allowed for Infant Records
variables.definition: ADate value from SCDM Encounter table, for mother's delivery encounter. Missing for infant-only records.

variables.name: DDate
variables.missing_allowed: Missing only allowed for Mother records
variables.definition: DDate value from SCDM Encounter table, for mother's delivery encounter. Missing for infant-only records.

variables.name: CPatID
variables.missing_allowed: Missing only allowed for Mother records
variables.definition: Arbitrary person-level identifier. Used to link across tables. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table. Must match infant PatID value in all other SCDM tables. Missing for mother/delivery only records.

variables.name: CBirth_Date
variables.missing_allowed: Missing only allowed for Mother records
variables.definition: Infant Birth_Date value from SCDM Demographic table. Missing for mother/delivery-only records.

variables.name: Sex
variables.missing_allowed: Missing only allowed for Mother records
variables.definition: Infant Sex value from SCDM Demographic table. Blank for mother/ delivery-only records.

variables.name: CEnr_Start
variables.missing_allowed: Missing only allowed for Mother records
variables.definition: Earliest Enr_Start from Enrollment table. Missing for mother/delivery-only records.

variables.name: Birth_Type
variables.missing_allowed: Missing only allowed for Infant Records

values.description: Conflicting code(s) for number of live births

variables.definition: Based upon ICD-9-CM/ICD-10-CM codes in the health plan data for the delivery admission. Missing for infant-only records.

variables.name: Birth_Type_Primes
variables.missing_allowed: Missing only allowed for Infant Records
variables.definition: Multiplication of all prime numbers assigned to all instances of Birth_Type found in delivery codes within the selected encounter, as follows: Prime = Birth_Type: 2 = Birth_Type of 0, 3 = Birth_Type of 1, 5 = Birth_Type of 2, 7 = Birth_Type of 3, 11 = Birth_Type of 4, 13 = Birth_Type of 5, 17 = Birth_Type of 8, 19 = Birth_Type of 9. This provides a record of all values of Birth_Type for the selected encounter. Missing for infant-only records.

variables.name: MatchMethod

values.description: health plan subscriber or family number
exact or probabilistic last name and address match based upon health plan administrative data
No subscriber/ family IDs available for linkage
No name/ address available for linkage
Neither subscriber/ family IDs nor name/ address available for linkage
no linkage made; any other reason

variables.definition: Prioritized method of linkage for mother-infant match, or reason for unlinked record: For linked records, prioritize so that only one method is listed: RE > SI > LA > BC > OT, For cases where a mother/ delivery is not linked to an infant OR an infant is not linked to a mother/delivery, the value of this variable should be one of N1, N2, N3, or NA only.

scdm_tables.description: The SCDM Patient-Reported Measures (PRM) Survey Table contains information on the surveys and questions administered. As data in this table is site-specific, questions may not be consistent across sites. Surveys are included only if they have been administered at least one time.

variables.name: PRM_Code
variables.definition: Unique integer identifier to a questionnaire type and subtype combination. Used to link across all PRM tables.

variables.name: SurveyID
variables.definition: Unique identifier to a collection of questions administered as a single survey. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see Reference Tables SAS-Lengths tab.

variables.name: Survey_Name
variables.definition: Survey name associated with a specific SurveyID. Multiple survey names may share the same SurveyID.

variables.name: Survey_Data_Source
variables.definition: Data source of the survey. For site-specific surveys which do not fall into one of the 3 primary categories, set value to LO. Useful for mapping back to source data.

variables.name: QuestionID
variables.definition: Unique identifier for a question. A question may have multiple question versions; see variable QuestionVerID.

variables.name: QuestionVerID
variables.definition: Unique sequence number of available versions of each question. If an edit is made to a question, the next published version of the question will contain a different sequence number than the first published version. All versions of each question that have been administered are included in the data. This may include questions that were updated because of spelling/ grammatical errors as well as rewording to increase precision in earlier versions.

variables.name: Question_Dt
variables.definition: Each combination of QuestionID/ QuestionVerID has a unique date of publication.

variables.name: Question_Text
variables.definition: Source question text. If there is a response to the question in the data, the question is included.
variables.example: "Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by any of the following problems? Little interest or pleasure in doing things?"

scdm_tables.description: The Patient-Reported Measures (PRM) Response table contains all responses to surverys and questions listed in the PRM Survey table. This table does not contain a primary key due to occurrences of multiple surveys on the same date/time for a given person.

variables.name: PatID
variables.definition: Arbitrary person-level identifier. Used to link across tables. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: PatID is a pseudo-identifier with a consistent crosswalk to the true identifier retained by the source Data Partner. For analytical data sets requiring patient-level data, only the pseudo-identifier is used to link across all information belonging to a patient.

variables.name: EncounterID
variables.definition: Arbitrary encounter-level identifier. Used to link across the Encounter, Diagnosis, Procedure, Vital Signs, Mother-Infant Linkage, Inpatient Pharmacy, Inpatient Transfusion, Prescribing, and Patient-Reported Measure Response tables. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: The Encounter, Diagnosis, Procedure, Inpatient Transfusion, Inpatient Pharmacy, Vital Signs, Mother-Infant Linkage, Prescribing, and Patient-Reported Measure Response tables are linked by PatID and EncounterID.

variables.name: PRM_Code
variables.definition: Unique integer identifier to a questionnaire type and subtype combination. Used to link across all PRM tables.

variables.name: SurveyID
variables.definition: Unique identifier to a collection of questions administered as a single survey. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see Reference Tables SAS-Lengths tab.

variables.name: QuestionID
variables.definition: Unique identifier for a question. A question may have multiple question versions; see variable QuestionVerID.

variables.name: QuestionVerID
variables.definition: Unique sequence number of available versions of each question. If an edit is made to a question, the next published version of the question will contain a different sequence number than the first published version. All versions of each question that have been administered are included in the data. This may include questions that were updated because of spelling/ grammatical errors as well as rewording to increase precision in earlier versions.

variables.name: Response_Dt
variables.definition: Date associated with the entered response

variables.name: Response_Tm
variables.definition: Time associated with the entered response

variables.name: Response_Text
variables.definition: Source response text. Use the fewest number of bytes to contain all response values.

variables.name: Survey_Mode
variables.definition: How the survey question was administered.

scdm_tables.description: The SCDM Feature Engineering Table contains one record per derived feature.

variables.name: PatID
variables.definition: Arbitrary person-level identifier. Used to link across tables. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: PatID is a pseudo-identifier with a consistent crosswalk to the true identifier retained by the source Data Partner. For analytical data sets requiring patient-level data, only the pseudo-identifier is used to link across all information belonging to a patient.

variables.name: EncounterID
variables.definition: Arbitrary encounter-level identifier. Used to link across the Encounter, Diagnosis, Procedure, Vital Signs, Mother-Infant Linkage, Inpatient Pharmacy, Inpatient Transfusion, Prescribing, and Patient-Reported Outcomes Response tables. Use the fewest number of bytes necessary to hold all distinct values; see "SAS Lengths" Reference Table.
notes: 'EncounterID' is a pseudo-identifier with a consistent crosswalk to the true identifier retained by the source Data Partner. For features not linked to an explicit Sentinel encounter, the EncounterID may be left missing.

variables.name: FeatureID
variables.definition: Identifier used to link the record to a FE metadata table locally maintained by the Data Partner storing information such as "NLP Pipeline Name", "NLP Version", "Date NLP Run", NLP Model validation metadata, and / or settings.

variables.name: Feature_dt
variables.definition: Date of the recorded feature. Where no date is assigned to the feature itself, this should be populated with the date the note was entered.

variables.name: Feature
variables.values: Feature Engineering code or Standard Term
variables.definition: Remove decimal points, site specific suffixes and prefixes. Other codes should be listed as recorded in the source data.

variables.name: FE_CodeType
variables.definition: Feature Engineering code type. This field combined with the FE field should be used to capture any type of diagnosis or clinical concept available in the source data. Other code types may be added as new terminologies are used.

variables.name: ProviderID
variables.definition: Identifier for the individual provider who made the diagnosis. As with the PatID, the ProviderID identifier is a pseudo-identifier with a consistent crosswalk to the real identifier. If an individual provider cannot be identified, populate the ProviderID value with special missing value of .U. This variable links to the Provider table.

variables.name: Confidence
variables.definition: Confidence that the feature derived from source data is accurate. If uncertain, use N = Not-Assessed

variables.name: Feature_Status
variables.definition: Indicators used to modify the derived feature based on context in the source note. Where the feature is present for the individual patient on the feature date and no negating criteria exist, status should = A. Where the feature is historically present for the individual patient, status should = H. Where the feature is ruled out, status should = N. Where the feature is not associated with the patient, status should = X.
